methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official UMN pages from Writing Across the Curriculum, Teaching Support, the Provost's Office, and UMN Libraries.

methodsAttempted.query: site:umn.edu University of Minnesota generative AI approved tools privacy data security Copilot ChatGPT policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced OIT appropriate-use guidance, UMN Libraries GenAI guidance, University Marketing Communications guidance, and school-level dentistry policies.

methodsAttempted.query: site:umn.edu University of Minnesota AI research policy generative AI research data ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found OIT, UMN Libraries, University Marketing Communications, and School of Dentistry research-related sources; no separate system-wide research GenAI policy was used.
Verified official UMN subdomains used in this run: it.umn.edu, provost.umn.edu, teachingsupport.umn.edu, libguides.umn.edu, umarcomm.umn.edu, and dentistry.umn.edu.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found substantive UMN system-level or central-unit guidance from OIT, the Provost's Office, Teaching Support, UMN Libraries, and University Marketing Communications. Claims are limited to those official pages. A School of Dentistry didactic GenAI policy was documented but rejected for this system-level run because it is school-scoped and not evidence of a University of Minnesota System-wide policy.

artifacts.claimText: UMN OIT guidance says University users should safeguard data by uploading only allowed University data into appropriately licensed AI tools, and it distinguishes UMN-licensed tools from an individual ChatGPT license.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow OIT data-handling and licensed-tool claim; it does not assert that every listed tool is appropriate for every data class.
Approved as directly supported by OIT guidance on human verification and code review.
Approved as course-policy guidance from the Provost page; claim does not convert syllabus templates into a blanket prohibition.
Approved as student-facing Libraries guidance, scoped to checking course expectations and citing or acknowledging permitted AI use.
Approved as narrow FAQ guidance on course-specific expectations and instructor communication.
Approved only as marketing and communications guidance; it explicitly does not address academic student or faculty use.
Approved as a narrow OIT statement about researcher responsibility to understand relevant agency AI policies; not a detailed research-compliance policy.

artifacts.title: University of Minnesota staged GenAI policy source review
artifacts.summary: Staged review found official UMN guidance from OIT, the Provost's Office, IT FAQ, UMN Libraries, Teaching Support, and University Marketing Communications. Candidate claims cover licensed-tool and data-use limits, human verification of AI output, course-level instructor authorization, student citation and acknowledgement, instructor communication, communications-work guidance, and a narrow researcher responsibility note. A School of Dentistry policy was rejected for system-wide extraction because it is school-scoped.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The School of Dentistry GenAI policy was not generalized to the University of Minnesota System because its source text is scoped to the dental school environment.
No login-gated, paywalled, CAPTCHA-protected, or private UMN sources were accessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
